macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Apple unveils a major Siri overhaul powered by AI
items.summary: Apple announced a redesigned Siri at WWDC, describing it as more conversational, personalized, and capable across apps, with stronger privacy positioning and deeper integration across the iPhone ecosystem. Reporting and event coverage also indicate Apple is using Google Gemini technology under the hood for some of the new Apple Intelligence features.

items.title: Anthropic reportedly releases first public Mythos-class model, Claude Fable
items.summary: A post claims Anthropic has launched Claude Fable, described as its first publicly available Mythos-class model, with stronger performance than Claude Opus 4.8 but tighter safety limits in high-risk domains like cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry. The post says it is available immediately and carries higher pricing than prior flagship models.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Code leadership says the tool has become routine for development work
items.summary: A recent talk from Anthropic's Claude Code lead highlights how rapidly agentic coding tools are changing developer workflows, with the speaker saying he no longer prompts Claude manually. The commentary reflects the growing trend toward AI systems that can execute more of the coding process autonomously.

items.title: Google and NVIDIA highlight GPU-accelerated machine learning beyond LLMs
items.summary: A new Google/NVIDIA session showcases GPU acceleration for machine learning workflows in drug screening and tabular data science, underscoring continued industry emphasis on speeding up non-LLM AI workloads with specialized hardware. The discussion fits the broader trend of AI infrastructure expansion beyond chatbots and foundation models.

items.title: Yann LeCun discusses world models as a path toward next-generation AI
items.summary: In a recent talk, Yann LeCun argued that current neural networks remain insufficient for true intelligence and emphasized world models and predictive learning as a route to more capable AI. The remarks reinforce an active research trend focused on modeling latent structure and future states rather than only next-token prediction.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nintendo Direct reportedly revealed major Switch 2 game announcements
items.summary: A recap video claims a recent Nintendo Direct included announcements and release dates for titles such as Devil May Cry 5 on Switch 2, Deltarune Chapter 5 as a free update on June 24, Kingdom Hearts HD collections for Switch on October 8, and a Switch 2 edition of Xenoblade Chronicles Definitive Edition available immediately. The same recap also claims an Ocarina of Time remake for Switch 2, but that specific claim should be treated cautiously because it comes from a commentary video rather than an official Nintendo source.

items.title: Xbox content pull hints at continued platform strategy changes
items.summary: A gaming-news discussion says Xbox removed a Gears video that briefly featured a PS5 logo only 24 hours after announcing it as exclusive, highlighting ongoing uncertainty around Xbox platform plans. The same discussion also references Project Helix and broader pipeline concerns, but those details are presented as commentary rather than a formal corporate announcement.

items.title: FromSoftware's The Duskbloods gets closed network test window
items.summary: A trailer breakdown reports that The Duskbloods will receive a closed network test sometime this summer, with commentary suggesting it may happen in July. This appears to be one of the main upcoming game-service announcements discussed in the past day.

items.title: Kingdom Hearts cloud versions are being delisted on Switch
items.summary: A gaming-news segment says the Nintendo Switch cloud versions of Kingdom Hearts are being delisted today, making it a notable storefront and distribution change. The discussion also notes that Kingdom Hearts HD 1.5+2.5 Remix is expected on Nintendo Switch on October 8.

items.title: Final Fantasy XIV early access timing discussed in Nintendo Direct recap
items.summary: The Nintendo Direct recap claims Final Fantasy XIV Online early access on Nintendo platforms is scheduled for August 2026, alongside other late-2026 release windows. This is one of the more notable industry updates mentioned in the recent roundup.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
